ai_chat.query: can you please put it to 3 paragraphs

response.content: Bryn Bower, a city with notable engineering industries, is grappling with groundwater contamination due to Exafluoran leakage from a Hallman Inc. facility. Despite substantial cleanup efforts costing $25 million over three decades, contamination persists, affecting the water supply of many private wells. The situation has escalated into complex legal and financial negotiations between the city and Hallman Inc.

Recently, Hallman proposed a $19 million settlement to the city to connect affected homes to the municipal water system, in exchange for the city absorbing future liabilities from the contamination. This proposal, although financially attractive, has divided the community, as reflected in vigorous debates during a recent contentious town hall meeting. Stakeholders are concerned about long-term impacts, trust in Hallman’s commitment, and the precedents set by accepting the deal.

response.title: Emotions Run High at Townhall Meeting Over Hallman Plume Scandal
response.description: The proposed settlement between Hallman Inc. and Bryn Bower has sparked fierce debate among residents, reflecting a community deeply divided over the best course of action for addressing the ongoing Exafluoran contamination issue.

response.content: Bryn Bower, a city impacted by the industrial solvent Exafluoran, faces significant environmental challenges from past disposal practices by Hallman Inc. A proposal by Hallman Inc. aims to address part of the ongoing contamination issues.

Hallman Inc. offered $19 Million to connect about 400 homes to municipal water, dropping all lawsuits and transferring future liabilities related to contamination outside the Hallman property to the city.

response.title: Map of Bryn Bower indicating the contaminated areas
response.description: The map shows that nearly 30% of Bryn Bower, including downtown and Bryn East, is under a water extraction prohibition due to >20ppb contamination of Exafluoran.

response.content: The cleaning effort, including the use of UV/peroxide treatment, has removed around 40% of the toxin, but further efforts might be cost-prohibitive.
The leaked document suggests ongoing high cleanup costs, projecting over $300M required to lower contamination levels across the board.

response.title: Hallman Cleanup Cost Estimates

response.content: The local community response to Hallman’s proposal was mixed, with intense debates and opposition during a townhall meeting.
Residents expressed concerns over property values, lack of trust in Hallman, and a diverse range of personal impacts from the contamination.

response.description: The emotional and contentious townhall meeting showcased strong public opinion, with arguments ranging from economic concerns to personal health and safety issues due to the Exafluoran contamination.

response.content: Despite the proposal, a significant number of residents are opposed, particularly the Clean Bryn Bower Initiative (CBBI), which insists on a comprehensive cleanup by Hallman without transferring liability to taxpayers.
